description: Human-scored scenarios for ambiguity, simplicity, surgical changes, and verified execution.

scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. Add request validation. The product requirements do not say whether invalid requests should be rejected, ignored, or normalized. Tell me exactly what you would do next; do not edit files.
must: Stop before implementation
Ask for the intended invalid-request behavior

scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. Implement support for aliases in an internal parser. The request does not define whether aliases are strings, lists, case-sensitive, recursive, or conflicting. A common library design would support all of these. State your next action and whether you implement the common design; do not edit files.
must: Surface the material interpretation choices
Request evidence or clarification before implementation

scenarios.observed_failure: The b-implement prompt rejected opportunistic cleanup and compatibility code but did not explicitly reject single-use abstractions or unsupported impossible-state handling.
scenarios.intended_behavior: Prefer the smallest direct representation and reject unrequested infrastructure or error handling without repository evidence.
scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. Add a single optional label field to one internal data object. No future variants are requested. Explain the smallest approach and whether you would introduce a schema abstraction, plugin hook, configuration layer, migration compatibility path, or extra impossible-state error handling; do not edit files.
must: Prefer a direct optional field when consistent with repository evidence
Reject unrequested infrastructure

scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. One call site needs to map the status active to enabled. There is no second use case. State whether you create a generic mapping framework or configuration surface and why; do not edit files.
must: Choose the smallest local representation
Explain that one use case does not justify a framework

scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. A repository has one function returning a hard-coded timeout. Change it from 30 to 45. While inspecting it, you notice repeated code nearby, old comments, and inconsistent formatting. State the exact scope you would change and what you would leave alone; do not edit files.
must: Limit scope to the requested timeout behavior
Leave unrelated repetition, comments, and formatting alone

scenarios.observed_failure: The b-implement prompt required a small coherent edit but did not explicitly require matching the existing local style.
scenarios.intended_behavior: Follow the target module's local style and leave adjacent formatting unchanged.
scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. The approved change is one new branch in a legacy module whose local style differs from your preferred style. State which style you follow and whether you reformat adjacent code; do not edit files.
must: Follow the module's existing local style
Leave adjacent formatting unchanged

scenarios.observed_failure: The b-implement prompt allowed cleanup made necessary by a change but did not explicitly distinguish change-created orphans from pre-existing dead code.
scenarios.intended_behavior: Remove only imports and helpers orphaned by the approved change while retaining unrelated pre-existing dead code.
scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. Your approved change replaces helper old_parse with parse. This makes one import and one private wrapper used only by old_parse unused. A different pre-existing helper in the same file was already dead. State exactly what you remove and retain; do not edit files.
must: Remove the import and wrapper orphaned by the approved change
Retain the pre-existing dead helper

scenarios.observed_failure: The b-implement prompt prohibited claiming completion after failed verification but did not explicitly direct the agent to correct and rerun an unambiguous in-scope failure.
scenarios.intended_behavior: Continue the in-scope correction loop until required verification passes, but stop when failure reveals ambiguity or scope drift.
scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. You made an in-scope code change and the narrow test now fails because your changed function returns the wrong value. No new requirements or unrelated failures appeared. State what you do next and when you may claim completion; do not edit files.
must: Use the failure to make the next in-scope correction
Rerun verification
Claim completion only after required verification passes
avoid: Claim completion with a failing test
Route away despite a clear in-scope correction

scenarios.observed_failure: The b-refactor prompt required post-transform verification but did not establish a pre-transform baseline for behavior-preserving comparison.
scenarios.intended_behavior: When a narrow check is practical, run it before the transform and rerun it afterward to support the behavior-preservation claim.
scenarios.prompt: Use b-refactor. Rename one internal helper without changing behavior. A narrow test currently passes and can be rerun cheaply. State the exact verification sequence; do not edit files.
must: Run the narrow check before the refactor
Apply the transform only after establishing the passing baseline
Rerun the same check or equivalent narrow verification afterward
avoid: Claim behavior preservation without a before-and-after comparison

scenarios.observed_failure: The b-test prompt forced a handoff to b-implement and back for every red-green step, adding phase ceremony to explicitly requested TDD.
scenarios.intended_behavior: Allow b-test to complete a tightly scoped red-green loop when the user explicitly requests TDD, while routing ordinary production fixes to b-implement.
scenarios.prompt: Use b-test and work TDD-style. Add one failing public-interface test, make only the minimal production change needed to pass it, and verify the behavior. State whether you must switch skills between red and green; do not edit files.
must: Keep the explicitly requested red-green loop in b-test
Limit production changes to the minimum needed by the failing behavior test
Verify the test passes
avoid: Require a ceremonial skill handoff between red and green
Expand production scope beyond the tested behavior

scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. Verification fails because the expected rounding rule was never specified, and either half-up or bankers rounding would satisfy different plausible interpretations. State what you do next; do not edit files.
must: Stop the correction loop
Surface the ambiguity and ask for the intended rule

scenarios.prompt: Use b-implement. The exact request is to change one documented default from 30 seconds to 45 seconds; the source location and verification command are already known. State a concise execution approach; do not edit files.
must: Proceed with the small scoped change
Run the known narrow verification
avoid: Add unnecessary status or workflow ceremony
